title: 10 best herbal creams for irritable bowel syndrome

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with irritable bowel syndrome because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. This reduces inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms such as bloating and abdominal pain associated with IBS. Additionally, licorice creams can also help to soothe and calm the digestive system, promoting regular bowel movements and reducing spasms. By providing relief from these symptoms, herbal licorice creams can improve overall quality of life for individuals suffering from IBS.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin, which helps to reduce inflammation in the gut and alleviate symptoms such as abdominal pain, bloating, and diarrhea.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in licorice cream help protect the gut lining from damage caused by free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and promoting a healthy gut environment that can mitigate IBS symptoms.

Soften and stabilize gut mucosa: Licorice extract has been shown to soften and stabilize the mucus layer in the gut, which can help reduce irritation and inflammation in the gut lining, leading to relief from IBS symptoms like abdominal pain and cramping.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making them effective in reducing symptoms of IBS.
Barks: Rich in flavonoids and polyphenols, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate digestive issues associated with IBS.
Seeds: Contain glyasperin, a compound that has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making them beneficial for soothing IBS symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Grind 50 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the powdered root with 100 grams of beeswax, 200 grams of coconut oil, and 100 grams of shea butter in a heat-proof bowl.
Melt the mixture over low heat for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the mixture reaches a temperature of 180°f.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to around 140°f, then slowly pour it into sterilized cream jars while whisking continuously to avoid introducing air bubbles.
Allow the creams to set at room temperature for 2 hours before use or storing in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with irritable bowel syndrome because it has a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing symptoms such as bloating, cramping, and diarrhea. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe the gut lining, reducing inflammation and irritation. Additionally, lemon balm's relaxant properties can help alleviate stress and anxiety, common triggers of IBS episodes. By promoting a sense of calm and relaxation, lemon balm creams can help regulate bowel movements and ease symptoms associated with irritable bowel syndrome.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Lemon balm cream helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping associated with IBS, allowing for easier digestion and reduced discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory property: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce inflammation in the gut, which can contribute to symptoms of IBS such as abdominal pain, bloating, and diarrhea or constipation.

Soothing and calming effect: The calming properties of lemon balm can help to reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers for IBS symptoms, promoting a sense of relaxation and reducing overall discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing digestive issues and reducing inflammation associated with IBS.
Leaves: Containing rosmarinic acid, a powerful antioxidant that helps calm digestive discomforts and reduces symptoms of IBS.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried melissa officinalis flowers and crush them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered melissa officinalis with 50ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 hours under gentle heat or in a warm water bath to allow the active compounds to infuse.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids and reserving the infused oil.
Add 20g of beeswax to the infused oil and stir until melted and fully incorporated using a double boiler or microwave-safe bowl.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with irritable bowel syndrome because it soothes the digestive system, alleviating symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and abdominal pain. The natural oils in peppermint, menthol, and eucalyptus relax the muscles in the intestines, reducing spasms and inflammation that can exacerbate IBS symptoms. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties help to calm the gut and reduce irritation, allowing for smoother digestion and bowel movements. This natural remedy provides a gentle and effective solution for managing IBS symptoms without harsh medication.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Peppermint cream's antispasmodic compounds help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps associated with IBS symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in peppermint creams reduce inflammation in the gut, which can contribute to IBS symptoms such as bloating, abdominal pain, and changes in bowel habits.

Gastroprotective property: Peppermint cream's gastroprotective properties help soothe and protect the lining of the stomach and intestines from irritation and damage, reducing symptoms like nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in menthol, which has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making them effective in reducing IBS symptoms like cramps and diarrhea.

Buds: Peppermint buds contain high levels of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help alleviate IBS symptoms such as bloating and abdominal pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh peppermint leaves and wash them gently with distilled water.
Steam distill the peppermint leaves using a clevenger apparatus for 4 hours.
Mix 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil with 10 milliliters of distilled water and 5 milliliters of witch hazel extract.
Add 1 milliliter of the peppermint essential oil to the mixture and stir well for 30 seconds.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and store in a cool dark place for 24 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with irritable bowel syndrome because they soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The anti-inflammatory properties of aloe vera gel help to ease abdominal pain, cramping, and bloating associated with IBS. Additionally, aloe creams can help to regulate bowel movements, improving stool consistency and frequency. By providing relief from these symptoms, aloe creams offer a natural solution for managing the uncomfortable and often debilitating effects of irritable bowel syndrome.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms like abdominal pain and cramping associated with IBS.
Antioxidant properties: Aloe vera's antioxidants, including vitamins C and E, help neutralize free radicals that can damage gut lining and contribute to IBS symptoms, such as bloating and gas.

Gut-soothing mucilages: The thick, gel-like substance in aloe vera contains mucilages that can coat and soothe the gut lining, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by food intolerance or other factors associated with IBS.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation associated with IBS.
Leaves: Containing a high concentration of polysaccharides, leaves provide natural anti-inflammatory and soothing properties to calm the gut and relieve symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves for their gel and juice, taking care not to damage the plant.
Combine 100ml of aloe vera gel with 20ml of coconut oil and 10ml of olive oil in a heat-resistant bowl.
Add 5g of dried ginger powder, 2g of turmeric powder, and 1g of activated charcoal powder to the mixture.
Heat the mixture at 40°c for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally, then let it cool and refrigerate for 2 hours.
Whip the cooled mixture using an electric mixer until it forms a smooth and creamy texture suitable for application.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Littleleaf linden creams helps with irritable bowel syndrome because of its unique combination of soothing herbal extracts. The cream's active ingredients, including tinctures of littleleaf linden and other calming herbs, work together to ease inflammation and discomfort in the digestive tract. By reducing symptoms such as bloating, cramping, and abdominal pain, the cream helps to promote a sense of relaxation and calmness in the body, allowing individuals with irritable bowel syndrome to experience greater comfort and manageability.

Antispasmodic property: The antispasmodic properties of the cream help relax smooth muscle contractions in the digestive tract, relieving cramps, bloating, and discomfort associated with IBS.

Soothing and protective property: The soothing and protective compounds in the cream help calm irritated mucous membranes in the gut, reducing inflammation and discomfort, and promoting healing and recovery from IBS episodes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which help to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation associated with IBS.
Barks: Contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps to alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality tilia cordata flowers and leaves for extraction.
Dry the plant material using shade or oven drying methods.
Extract the bioactive compounds using ethanol and hot water.
Mix the extract with a base cream containing emulsifying waxes and preservatives.
Package and store the finished cream at room temperature or refrigerate for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn creams helps with irritable bowel syndrome because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and soothing properties. The cream's flavonoids, such as quercetin and rutin, work to calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Additionally, hawthorn's antispasmodic effects help to relax the muscles in the intestine, easing cramping and spasms that can exacerbate IBS symptoms. By providing a calming and soothing environment, hawthorn creams may help alleviate symptoms of IBS, promoting a sense of relief and comfort for those affected by this chronic condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Hawthorn cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and polyphenols, reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms like abdominal pain, bloating, and diarrhea.

Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic compounds in hawthorn cream relax the smooth muscles in the digestive tract, calming muscle spasms and reducing cramping, which can help alleviate IBS symptoms such as abdominal pain and changes in bowel movements.

Digestive-soothing properties: Hawthorn cream's mucilages and saponins soothe the digestive lining, reducing irritation and inflammation, and promoting a healthy balance of gut bacteria, which helps to regulate bowel function and reduce IBS symptoms like constipation or diarrhea.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hawthorn roots have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in soothing digestive issues associated with IBS.
Barks: The barks of the hawthorn plant contain flavonoids that help reduce inflammation and improve gut motility, alleviating symptoms of IBS.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried crataegus monogyna leaves in sufficient quantity for the desired batch size of cream preparation.
Grind 20g of dried crataegus monogyna leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground powder with 100ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and 20ml of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for approximately 30 minutes or until the wax is fully incorporated and melted.
Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature before transferring to airtight containers for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blueberry creams helps with irritable bowel syndrome because they provide a soothing and calming effect on the digestive system. The herbal ingredients in these creams, such as peppermint oil and chamomile, help to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, reducing spasms and cramping associated with IBS. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of blueberries may also help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the gut, leading to improved digestion and reduced symptoms of bloating, abdominal pain, and irregular bowel movements.
remedy_properties: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate IBS symptoms such as bloating and abdominal pain.

Fiber-rich polysaccharides: The high fiber content of herbal blueberry creams helps regulate bowel movements, soothe the gut lining, and promote a healthy balance of gut bacteria, reducing symptoms of constipation, diarrhea, and abdominal cramps associated with IBS.

Phytochemicals: The various phytochemicals present in herbal blueberry creams, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation in the gut, alleviate symptoms of IBS, and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, blueberry roots help soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation associated with IBS.
Fruits: The anthocyanins present in blueberry fruits have been shown to have a relaxing effect on the gut, reducing symptoms of IBS such as bloating and cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of vaccinium myrtillus berries, drying them for at least 2 hours in a single layer on paper towels.
Blend the dried vaccinium myrtillus berries into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 20g of the vaccinium myrtillus powder with 10ml of jojoba oil, 5ml of beeswax, and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax melts and the mixture thickens to a creamy consistency.
Remove from heat, let cool, and pour into sterilized glass jars or tubes, allowing the cream to set at room temperature for at least 2 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with irritable bowel syndrome because its mucilages soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation. The herbal extract's gel-like properties help to coat and protect the gut lining, allowing for easier digestion and absorption of nutrients. Additionally, marshmallow cream's demulcent properties help to bulk up stool, reducing symptoms of constipation and diarrhea often associated with IBS.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Acts as a natural anti-inflammatory agent, reducing inflammation in the gut and alleviating symptoms such as abdominal pain and cramping.
Mucilage: Forms a protective barrier on the mucous membranes of the digestive tract, soothing and calming irritated tissues, and helping to repair damaged gut lining.

Saponins: Have prebiotic properties, promoting the growth of beneficial bacteria in the gut, which can help restore the balance of gut flora and reduce symptoms such as bloating and abdominal discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and protects the gut lining, helping to alleviate symptoms of IBS.
Barks: Contain a high concentration of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties and help to calm irritated bowels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh marshmallow roots and dry them according to your preferred method for a week or two.
Grind dried marshmallow roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 10 grams of powdered marshmallow root with 100 ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes stirring occasionally while monitoring the temperature at 60 to 80 degrees celsius.
Remove from heat and allow the mixture to cool and infuse for 2 hours before straining through a cheesecloth and storing in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with irritable bowel syndrome because its unique combination of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and soothing properties provides relief from digestive discomfort. The cream's ability to calm and relax the gut lining helps reduce inflammation and irritation, which can contribute to IBS symptoms such as bloating, cramping, and abdominal pain. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing effects help maintain a healthy gut barrier, allowing for proper digestion and absorption of nutrients, further alleviating symptoms associated with IBS.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like salicin, which can help reduce inflammation and discomfort in the digestive tract associated with IBS symptoms.

Antioxidant properties: Maiden hair tree extracts have antioxidant properties that can help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the gut, which can contribute to IBS symptoms like abdominal pain and bloating.

Soothing and calming effects: The creams' anti-irritant properties can provide a soothing and calming effect on the digestive system, helping to alleviate symptoms of IBS such as diarrhea, constipation, and cramping.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome.
Barks: The bark of the maidenhair tree contains compounds like berberine and palmatine, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects that may help soothe digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Combine the powdered ginkgo biloba with 20g of beeswax pellets, 10g of coconut oil, and 5g of shea butter in a double boiler setup over low heat.
Heat the mixture while stirring occasionally until the beeswax is fully melted and the temperature reaches 180°f, which takes about 30 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to room temperature before whipping it with an electric mixer for 5 minutes to create a smooth and creamy texture.
Pour the cooled ginkgo biloba cream into sterilized glass jars, store them in a cool dark place, and allow them to mature for at least 2 weeks before using them on the skin.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with irritable bowel syndrome because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The apigenin in chamomile relaxes the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and spasms that can lead to cramps, bloating, and diarrhea. Additionally, chamomile's calming effects help to reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers for IBS symptoms. By applying topical creams or ointments, patients may experience relief from discomfort and improve their overall quality of life.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of IBS like abdominal pain and cramping.

Antispasmodic properties: The flavonoids present in chamomile, particularly apigenin, have a relaxing effect on the muscles in the digestive tract, helping to relieve spasms and cramps that are common in IBS.

Anxiolytic properties: Chamomile's anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) compounds, such as apigenin and linalool, can help to calm the nervous system, which can contribute to IBS symptoms like anxiety, stress, and digestive dysfunction.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin, a flavonoid with anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe digestive issues associated with IBS.
Flowers: Contain azulene, an essential oil that calms the gut and reduces inflammation, providing relief from IBS symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Sterilize glass jars and utensils in boiling water for at least 15 minutes to ensure cleanliness.
Combine 20 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with 100 milliliters of boiling distilled water to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the herbal infusion through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bowl, retaining only the liquid.
Mix 50 grams of coconut oil with the strained herbal infusion and stir until the oil is fully incorporated at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Pour the resulting mixture into the sterilized glass jars, leaving about one centimeter headspace, and seal tightly before allowing it to cool and set for at least 4 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with irritable bowel syndrome because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The cream's natural properties also help to calm spasms in the intestines, easing symptoms of bloating, cramping, and abdominal pain. Additionally, stinging nettle has been shown to increase gut motility, helping to regulate bowel movements and reduce diarrhea or constipation. This natural remedy provides a gentle, non-invasive way to manage IBS symptoms and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Stinging nettle contains bioactive compounds like caffeic acid, chlorogenic acid, and quercetin, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation in the gut and alleviate IBS symptoms.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in stinging nettle creams, such as vitamin C, beta-carotene, and lutein, can help neutralize free radicals and protect the intestinal lining from oxidative damage, reducing inflammation and improving bowel function.

Soothing and anti-spasmodic effects: Stinging nettle's soothing properties can help relax the smooth muscle in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps associated with IBS, while its anti-spasmodic compounds like scopoletin can help alleviate symptoms of diarrhea, constipation, and bloating.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, stinging nettle roots help soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation associated with IBS.
Seeds: Containing essential fatty acids and antioxidants, stinging nettle seeds promote healthy gut flora and alleviate symptoms of IBS.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh nettle leaves urtica dioica in amounts of approximately 500 grams for subsequent processing.
Dry the nettle leaves using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 6 hours or air drying for 7 days.
Blend one part dried nettle leaves with one part coconut oil and two parts beeswax in a heat-resistant container.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 70°c for 30 minutes stirring occasionally to combine and melt.
Allow the mixture to cool slightly then whip it using an electric mixer until it reaches a creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with irritable bowel syndrome because they contain curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound found in turmeric. Curcumin has been shown to reduce inflammation in the gut, which is often exacerbated in individuals with IBS. By decreasing inflammation, curcumin may help alleviate symptoms such as abdominal pain, bloating, and diarrhea or constipation, thereby providing relief for those suffering from this condition. Additionally, curcumin's antioxidant properties can also aid in repairing damaged gut lining and promoting overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Turmeric's curcuminoids, such as curcumin and demethoxycurcumin, have potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the gut and alleviate symptoms like abdominal pain and cramping.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant compounds in turmeric creams, including vitamin C, vitamin E, and beta-carotene, can help neutralize free radicals in the digestive tract, reducing oxidative stress and promoting overall gut health.

Gastroprotective effects: Turmeric's bioactive compounds have been shown to protect the stomach lining from damage caused by acid reflux, reducing symptoms like heartburn and bloating, which are common in IBS patients.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of turmeric contain a high concentration of curcuminoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate symptoms of IBS such as abdominal pain and bloating.
Roots: Turmeric roots are rich in volatile oils and phenolic compounds that have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve gut motility, making them useful for treating IBS symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Dry the curcuma longa rhizomes in a warm oven at 150°f for 2 hours or until brittle.
Grind 20 grams of dried curcuma longa into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Melt 100 grams of coconut oil and combine with 5 grams of beeswax, stirring until fully incorporated.
Add the ground curcuma longa powder to the melted coconut oil mixture and stir for 10 minutes or until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a glass container and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or until solidified before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary creams helps with irritable bowel syndrome because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation. The essential oils in rosemary also help to relax the muscles in the gut, reducing spasms and cramping that often accompany IBS symptoms. Additionally, rosemary has been shown to improve digestion and alleviate bloating, discomfort, and abdominal pain associated with IBS, offering relief from its uncomfortable symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Rosemary's antioxidant compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the digestive tract, which can alleviate IBS symptoms like abdominal pain and bloating.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds, including carnosic acid, reduce inflammation in the gut lining, which can ease IBS symptoms like diarrhea, constipation, and abdominal pain.

Gastroprotective Properties: Rosemary's gastroprotective properties, including its ability to increase mucous production and improve gastric motility, help soothe and protect the digestive tract, reducing the risk of inflammation and injury.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the gut, making it an effective ingredient for soothing digestive issues associated with IBS.
Barks: The barks of rosemary contain antioxidants and flavonoids that can help alleviate symptoms of IBS by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the morning and rinse them gently with clean water.
Combine 20g of dried rosmarinus officinalis powder with 50ml of cold-pressed coconut oil and blend for 10 minutes.
Heat a mixture of 30ml of sweet almond oil and 15ml of beeswax at 70°c to 80°c for 30 minutes.
Add the blended rosmarinus officinalis mixture to the heated oil mixture and stir until fully incorporated after 5 minutes.
Pour the final cream into sterilized glass jars and store in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle creams helps with irritable bowel syndrome because of its unique ability to soothe and calm the digestive tract. The cream's active ingredient, silymarin, has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation and irritation in the gut, alleviating symptoms such as bloating, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. remedy_properties: Silymarin: Helps to reduce inflammation in the gut, which can contribute to IBS symptoms such as abdominal pain and cramping, by inhibiting pro-inflammatory cytokines and promoting antioxidant activity.
Flavonoids: Exhibit anti-spasmodic properties, which can help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of IBS such as bloating, abdominal pain, and diarrhea or constipation.

Phytosterols: Have a prebiotic effect, meaning they feed beneficial bacteria in the gut, promoting a healthy gut microbiome and potentially reducing symptoms of IBS such as bloating and changes in bowel movements.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in silymarin, a flavonoid complex that helps protect the gut lining and reduce inflammation, making it an effective ingredient for IBS creams.
Seeds: Containing a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, milk thistle seeds can help soothe digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of IBS.
remedy_recipe: Harvest silybum marianum leaves and flowers at dawn on a dry day for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm oven at 50°c for 24 hours or use a desiccant.
Combine 200g of dried silybum marianum with 400ml of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil and heat gently for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean container discarding the solids and store in an airtight container at room temperature.
Allow the infused oil to mature for 6 weeks before whipping with beeswax and vitamin e oil to create a smooth cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with irritable bowel syndrome because they contain a combination of anti-inflammatory and soothing compounds that ease digestive discomfort. The cream's active ingredients, such as taraxasterol and taraxeryl acetate, help reduce inflammation in the gut and promote relaxation of the digestive tract muscles. This relief from inflammation and tension can alleviate symptoms like abdominal pain, bloating, and diarrhea, providing natural relief for individuals suffering from irritable bowel syndrome.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol, reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which alleviates symptoms like bloating and abdominal pain.

Prebiotic effects: The inulin fiber content in dandelion creams helps feed good bacteria in the gut, promoting a balanced gut microbiome, which can alleviate constipation, diarrhea, and other IBS symptoms.
Digestive soothing properties: Dandelion's bitter compounds stimulate digestive juices and help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping associated with IBS.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in inulin, a prebiotic fiber that helps regulate gut bacteria and soothe digestive issues.
Fruits: Contain antioxidants and flavonoids that help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of IBS.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried taraxacum officinale leaves in amounts of 20-30 grams for every 100 milliliters of cream base.
Combine the harvested leaves with 100 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat gently for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth to extract the herbal properties and discard the solids after 10 minutes.
Mix the filtered oil with 200 milliliters of beeswax, 20 grams of shea butter, and 10 drops of lavender essential oil in a heat-resistant bowl.
Stir the mixture constantly until the beeswax and shea butter have melted completely then pour into glass jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with irritable bowel syndrome because they contain natural compounds that soothe and calm the digestive tract. The essential oils in fennel, such as anethole, have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with IBS symptoms like bloating, cramps, and abdominal pain. Additionally, fennel's carminative properties help to expel gas and alleviate constipation, providing relief from persistent digestive issues. Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic compounds in fennel cream relax the muscles in the intestines and reduce spasms, which helps to relieve constipation, diarrhea, and abdominal cramping common in IBS sufferers.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel cream's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms such as abdominal pain, bloating, and bowel changes associated with IBS.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and soothing digestive issues associated with IBS.
Buds: Fennel buds contain flavonoids that have been shown to have prebiotic properties, helping to regulate the gut microbiome and alleviate symptoms of IBS.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers, ensuring proper identification and cleanliness.
Dry the harvested plant material using shade or low-temperature drying methods for 7 days.
Combine 20 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare with 100 milliliters of organic jojoba oil and 10 milliliters of coconut oil.
Simmer the mixture in a double boiler at 60°c for 30 minutes, allowing for infusion and extraction of active compounds.
Strain the resulting liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter, discarding solids and transferring the cream to sterilized containers for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal creams helps with irritable bowel syndrome because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated bowels. The cream's active compound, berberine, reduces inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms such as abdominal pain, bloating, and diarrhea. Additionally, goldenseal's antimicrobial properties help to restore balance to the gut microbiome, promoting a healthy digestion and reducing inflammation in the colon.

Berberin alkaloids: These alkaloids have been shown to relax the smooth muscle in the digestive tract, which can help alleviate cramping, diarrhea, and constipation associated with IBS.
Antioxidant properties: Goldenseal's antioxidant compounds may help protect the gut lining from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to inflammation and worsen IBS symptoms.

Rhyzomes: Contain mucilages that help lubricate the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather and clean fresh hydrastis canadensis roots, measuring approximately one pound.
Dry the cleaned hydrastis canadensis roots in a warm oven at 150°f for two hours.
Grind dried hydrastis canadensis roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine three tablespoons of hydrastis canadensis powder with six tablespoons of coconut oil and six tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax melts and the cream reaches a consistency suitable for application.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with irritable bowel syndrome because they contain echinacea, a natural anti-inflammatory agent that soothes the digestive tract. The cream's cooling properties help to reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with IBS, such as abdominal cramps and diarrhea. Additionally, Kansas coneflower creams may also help to regulate bowel movements, reducing symptoms of constipation or loose stools. By addressing these underlying issues, Kansas coneflower creams provide relief from the chronic discomfort and distress of irritable bowel syndrome.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The cream's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the gut, which is a common underlying factor in IBS symptoms.

Antioxidant activity: Antioxidants in the cream can neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and tissue damage, which may exacerbate IBS symptoms such as bloating and abdominal pain.

Gut-soothing flavonoids: The cream's flavonoid content, particularly quercetin and luteolin, have been shown to have a calming effect on the gut lining, reducing inflammation and improving digestion in individuals with IBS.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Kansas conefeower contain bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in soothing digestive issues associated with IBS.
Fruits: The fruits of the Kansas coneflower contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and discomfort caused by IBS.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality echinacea angustifolia roots in late summer or early fall to ensure optimal potency and flavor.
Dry the harvested roots using a food dehydrator set at 150°f for 12 hours or air dry them for 7-10 days to prevent mold growth.
Grind dried echinacea angustifolia roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill, taking care not to generate heat or friction.
Mix the powdered echinacea with 20% beeswax and 80% coconut oil in a double boiler, heating the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes until melted and well combined.
Pour the cooled herbal cream into sterilized glass jars and store them in the refrigerator at a temperature of 40°f or below to preserve potency and shelf life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with irritable bowel syndrome because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream contains calends, a compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, thereby alleviating symptoms such as bloating, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Additionally, the cream's emollient nature helps to protect and moisturize the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, allowing for easier digestion and reducing discomfort associated with IBS. This natural remedy provides relief from IBS symptoms without harsh chemicals or medications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold cream's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms such as bloating and abdominal pain associated with IBS.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of pot marigold cream help protect the gut lining from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to IBS symptoms like diarrhea or constipation.

Softpene and anti-spasmodic: Pot marigold's softpene and anti-spasmodic properties help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping that can occur with IBS, allowing for smoother digestion and bowel movements.
remedy_parts: Roots: Potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them effective in soothing digestive issues.
Seeds: Rich in antioxidants and essential fatty acids, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the gut.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality calendula officinalis flowers and dry them thoroughly in a warm, well-ventilated area for several days.
Grind the dried calendula flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the calendula powder with beeswax and coconut oil in a double boiler at low heat for 10 minutes stirring occasionally until melted and smooth.
Add vitamin e oil and aloe vera gel to the mixture and stir well to combine before pouring into sterilized containers for cooling and hardening.
Allow the cream to cool and harden completely before labeling and storing it in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for irritable bowel syndrome are topical products infused with natural herbs and botanicals that provide relief from symptoms associated with IBS. These creams work by soothing the digestive system, reducing inflammation, and promoting relaxation. Examples of herbal creams that help alleviate IBS symptoms include those containing peppermint, chamomile, and licorice root. By using these creams, individuals with IBS can experience reduced abdominal pain, bloating, and diarrhea, leading to improved quality of life and increased comfort in their daily activities.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat irritable bowel syndrome with herbal creams is by using soothing formulas that calm inflammation and ease digestive discomfort. Look for creams containing aloe vera, marshmallow root, or slippery elm, which have anti-inflammatory properties and can help reduce symptoms such as bloating, cramping, and diarrhea. Apply the cream topically to the affected area, following instructions carefully, to alleviate IBS symptoms and promote overall gut health.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to irritable bowel syndrome that are treated with herbal creams are hemorrhoids, anal fissures, and proctalgia. These conditions often present with symptoms of discomfort, itching, and inflammation in the rectal area. Herbal creams containing ingredients like witch hazel, aloe vera, and tea tree oil can provide relief by reducing swelling, soothing irritation, and promoting healing.
